Title: pseudo-GhFAD2-1 Is a lncRNA Involved in Regulating Cottonseed Oleic and Linoleic Acid Ratios and Seed Size in Gossypium hirsutum

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases oleic acid (C18:1) proportion, decreases linoleic acid (C18:2) proportion, lowers oil content, and increases seed size; knockout increases linoleic acid proportion and oil content but reduces seed size.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pGhFAD2-1 is a tandem-duplicated, noncoding homolog of GhFAD2-1D that is specifically expressed in middle to late seed development. 2. pGhFAD2-1 represses GhFAD2-1 at both transcriptional and translational levels, shifting cottonseed C18:1/C18:2 ratios toward higher oleic acid when overexpressed and toward higher linoleic acid when knocked out. 3. pGhFAD2-1 also affects seed size through a GhHDT1- and ABA-associated pathway, with reduced pGhFAD2-1 causing smaller seeds and elevated ABA.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RACE cloning; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; seed-specific overexpression; qRT-PCR; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; oil-content measurement; Western blot; ELISA enzyme activity assay; RNA pull-down; RIP; MST; dual-luciferase assay; RNA-seq; ATAC-seq
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specifically expressed in middle and late cottonseed development (20–50 DPA) at generally low levels.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Middle and late developing seeds (20–50 DPA).
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (detected by in situ hybridization for transcript in seed cotyledon and hypocotyl nuclei)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Seed-specific promoter for overexpression; GhU6.7 promoter for sgRNA expression in CRISPR construct
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Long non-coding RNA; tandem duplicate/pseudogene-derived lncRNA related to FAD2-1
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GhHDT1; GhRPS12; GhFAD2-1 mRNA (competitive relationship via RPS12, no direct pGhFAD2-1–GhFAD2-1 binding detected)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stage (20–60 DPA); exogenous ABA in ovule culture relevant to seed-size interpretation
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Acts with histone deacetylase GhHDT1 to modulate chromatin accessibility and transcription of GhFAD2-1 and ABA-related genes; pGhFAD2-1 also attenuates GhHDT1 self-feedback on its own promoter.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (30–60 DPA seeds, no public accession reported); ATAC-seq (30 and 40 DPA seeds, no public accession reported)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed oil nutritional quality; oleic acid improves oxidative stability whereas linoleic acid is a major polyunsaturated fatty acid in cottonseed oil.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout raises oil content and linoleic acid but reduces seed size and 100-seed weight; overexpression enlarges seeds but lowers oil content. Final oil yield improvement is limited because oil content and seed size effects oppose each other.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune cottonseed oleic/linoleic acid ratio and seed size, but not ideal alone for improving total oil yield because higher oil content and larger seed size are antagonistically coupled. Deployment may require stacking with seed-size or oil-yield genes to uncouple tradeoffs.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The previously annotated Gh_D13G2237 protein-coding model was experimentally refuted; pGhFAD2-1 lacks coding capacity despite sequence homology to GhFAD2-1D. Residual edited transcript (pGhFAD2-1mut) lost binding to GhHDT1 and failed to disrupt RPS12–GhFAD2-1 interaction.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Microsomal fatty acid desaturase 2 with conserved histidine motifs
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Unsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis; cottonseed oil biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oleic acid to linoleic acid desaturation branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream desaturation step in seed oil biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: oleic acid (C18:1), likely phosphatidylcholine-linked oleate in ER membranes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates oleic acid (C18:1) to linoleic acid (C18:2) in the endoplasmic reticulum.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step; described as a key gene/pivotal step for synthesis of polyunsaturated fatty acids and direct determinant of C18:2 content
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: developing and mature cottonseed; seed kernel oil
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher GhFAD2-1 expression/protein/activity promotes conversion of C18:1 to C18:2, increasing linoleic acid and reducing oleic acid proportion in cottonseed.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhFAD2-1A and GhFAD2-1D expression increased in pGhFAD2-1 knockout and decreased in pGhFAD2-1 overexpression lines. 2. GhFAD2-1 protein abundance and enzymatic activity in developing seeds tracked with these transcript changes, being highest in knockout lines. 3. Altered GhFAD2-1 activity explained reciprocal changes in cottonseed oleic and linoleic acid composition.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; RNA-seq; Western blot; ELISA enzyme activity assay; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; EMSA; Y1H; FISH co-localization
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter analyzed; promoter fragments used in EMSA and Y1H
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly accumulated/expressed in 30–50 DPA developing seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: 30–50 DPA developing seeds; GhFAD2-1A and GhFAD2-1D are active during oil accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major determinant of nutritional oil composition and polyunsaturated fatty acid content in cottonseed.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In this study, increased GhFAD2-1 activity associated with higher linoleic acid and lower oleic acid; system-level oil content effects are context-dependent and coupled to seed-size tradeoffs via pGhFAD2-1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering cottonseed fatty acid quality; lowering GhFAD2-1 or its effective translation would raise oleic acid, while increasing it raises linoleic acid. Trait deployment should consider oil content context and interaction with pGhFAD2-1-mediated seed-size effects.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GhFAD2-1 is highly conserved across Gossypium species and exists as single-copy homologs in A and D subgenomes with minimal divergence. No direct RNA-RNA interaction with pGhFAD2-1 was detected; regulation occurs through GhHDT1 recruitment and RPS12 competition.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of unsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis gene expression and ABA biosynthesis/signaling-linked seed development
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression via histone deacetylation/chromatin remodeling; autoregulatory negative feedback on its own promoter that is alleviated by pGhFAD2-1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GhFAD2-1A; GhFAD2-1D; GhNCED; GhABA3; GhSRK2E; GhPYR1; GhHDT1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds GhFAD2-1 promoters and represses GhFAD2-1 transcription; represses ABA biosynthesis/signaling-associated genes, thereby lowering ABA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in complex with lncRNA pGhFAD2-1 for recruitment to GhFAD2-1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback on its own transcription through promoter binding; pGhFAD2-1 attenuates this self-repression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller linking lncRNA signaling to FAD2 transcription and ABA-mediated seed-size regulation, but not alone sufficient to alter fatty acid composition at protein/metabolite levels when knocked down.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher GhHDT1 suppresses GhFAD2-1 transcription and ABA pathway genes; reducing GhHDT1 expression decreases seed size and increases ABA, but does not significantly change fatty acid composition due to translational buffering of GhFAD2-1.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhHDT1 physically interacts with pGhFAD2-1 and binds GhFAD2-1 promoters, supporting pGhFAD2-1-guided transcriptional repression of GhFAD2-1. 2. GhHDT1 is itself under negative feedback through promoter binding, and pGhFAD2-1 relieves this feedback to maintain GhHDT1 abundance. 3. GhHDT1 knockdown increases ABA-pathway gene expression and ABA accumulation, causing smaller seeds, while fatty acid composition remains largely unchanged.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA pull-down; RIP; subcellular localization; EMSA; Y1H; MST; dual-luciferase assay; RNAi knockdown; qRT-PCR; Western blot; hormone quantification; ATAC-seq
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression and protein accumulation gradually increased from 30 to 60 DPA in cottonseeds.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stage; TSA treatment referenced for chromatin accessibility comparison; ABA-associated seed development context
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: This gene is itself an epigenetic regulator; reducing GhHDT1 increases chromatin accessibility and expression of ABA-related genes such as GhABA2 and GhNCED1.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA regulates seed maturation and negatively affects seed growth; fatty acid ratio determines cottonseed oil quality.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockdown reduces seed size without improving fatty acid profile; manipulating GhHDT1 may therefore compromise seed development.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for dissecting chromatin-level control of seed development and ABA pathways, but not an optimal standalone target for improving cottonseed oil quality because altering GhHDT1 mainly affects seed size rather than fatty acid composition.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although GhHDT1 knockdown raised GhFAD2-1 transcripts, GhFAD2-1 protein/activity and C18:1/C18:2 were unchanged, likely due to pGhFAD2-1/RPS12-mediated translational competition buffering.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Translation of GhFAD2-1 mRNA affecting unsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Competitive RNA binding; pGhFAD2-1 sequesters RPS12 away from GhFAD2-1 mRNA to inhibit GhFAD2 protein synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binding to GhFAD2-1 mRNA supports its translation, whereas pGhFAD2-1 competes for RPS12 and reduces effective GhFAD2-1 translation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Translational gatekeeper for GhFAD2-1 output; enables pGhFAD2-1-mediated suppression of linoleic acid biosynthesis without direct RNA-RNA pairing.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Competitive binding of pGhFAD2-1 to GhRPS12 reduces GhFAD2-1 translation, lowering C18:2 synthesis and increasing C18:1 proportion.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhRPS12 was identified as a specific pGhFAD2-1-interacting protein by RNA pull-down and RIP. 2. MST showed GhRPS12 binds pGhFAD2-1 with higher affinity than GhFAD2-1 mRNA. 3. Addition of pGhFAD2-1 disrupts the GhRPS12–GhFAD2-1 mRNA interaction, providing a mechanism for translational repression of GhFAD2-1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA pull-down; RIP; recombinant protein purification; MST competition assay
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines nutritional balance of monounsaturated versus polyunsaturated fatty acids in cottonseed oil.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may broadly affect translation because RPS12 is a core ribosomal protein, implying possible pleiotropy if directly targeted.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Mechanistically important but likely poor direct engineering target because of essential ribosomal function; better leveraged indirectly through pGhFAD2-1-mediated competitive binding.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Edited residual pGhFAD2-1mut failed to alter GhRPS12–GhFAD2-1 mRNA binding, confirming sequence-dependent regulatory activity.
